reasoning:
Verdict: REJECT (21% confidence)

Module Scores:
  Sentinel (CWE/OWASP Web Application Security): 50/100 [Info risk] — 1 info
  Watchdog (OWASP LLM Top 10 / Cisco AI Threat Taxonomy): 99/100 [Info risk] — 1 info
  Guardian (NIST AI RMF / EU AI Act / UNICC Responsible AI): 4/100 [Critical risk] — 4 critical, 6 high, 4 medium, 2 low

Arbitration Triggers:
  • REJECT trigger — score below 30: Guardian (4/100)
  • REJECT trigger — critical findings in Guardian: [guardian-1] EU AI Act High-Risk Classification with Zero Conformity Controls — Autonomous Content Authenticity Decisions Affecting Public Discourse; [guardian-2] No Human Oversight Mechanism for Autonomous Content Moderation Decisions Affecting Freedom of Expression; [guardian-3] Complete Absence of Governance Documentation — No README, No Policies, No Operational Guidelines; [guardian-4] No Bias or Fairness Testing in ResNet Deepfake Detection and BERT Fact-Verification Pipelines

Disagreement Resolution:
  ⚠ Watchdog scored 99/100 (info risk) while Sentinel scored 50/100 (info risk) — Δ49 point divergence. Arbitration defers to the stricter assessment from Sentinel to maintain safety margins.
  ⚠ Sentinel scored 50/100 (info risk) while Guardian scored 4/100 (critical risk) — Δ46 point divergence. Arbitration defers to the stricter assessment from Guardian to maintain safety margins.
  ⚠ Watchdog scored 99/100 (info risk) while Guardian scored 4/100 (critical risk) — Δ95 point divergence. Arbitration defers to the stricter assessment from Guardian to maintain safety margins.

Confidence Factors:
  • -30% confidence: 3 disagreement(s) between modules
  • Base score: 51/100 average across 3 module(s)

--- LLM-Enhanced Council Narrative ---
Across the three assessments, there is strong agreement that the current evaluation is constrained by missing implementation detail: Sentinel and Watchdog both explicitly flag the absence of source code, and Guardian notes a wholesale lack of governance and technical documentation. This shared limitation underpins Sentinel’s and Watchdog’s largely informational ratings, while Guardian interprets the same opacity as an acute governance failure for what it classifies as a high-risk AI system. All modules converge on the idea that meaningful, concrete assurance — whether security, AI-specific, or regulatory — is impossible without substantially more transparency into the system’s code, data, and processes.

The main tension lies in how overall risk is framed. Watchdog scores the system very highly and low-risk from an LLM perspective because no LLMs or tool-using agents are detected, whereas Guardian treats the non-LLM AI components (ResNet deepfake detection and BERT fact-checking) as already posing critical risk to fundamental rights due to absent oversight, fairness testing, privacy controls, and auditability. Sentinel, meanwhile, remains cautious but limited to traditional web security concerns, rating only informational risk until code is available. Taken together, these views suggest that while LLM-specific threats may be out of scope today, the system is nonetheless in a high-risk category driven by governance and societal-impact failures, layered on top of an unassessed but potentially significant web-application attack surface. Future work should therefore prioritize exposing code and documentation, aligning severity across modules, and extending security analysis to cover both classic application vulnerabilities and the high-stakes harms Guardian anticipates from the system’s current operating model.

top_findings: No detectable LLM or tool-using AI integrations in VeriMedia AI codebase
watchdog.summary: VeriMedia AI is a media verification platform using vision (ResNet) and NLP (BERT) models, but no explicit LLM or LLM orchestration technologies (such as GPT-4o, Claude, Gemini, LangChain, or LlamaIndex) were detectable from the provided description, and no source code was available. Consequently, no concrete LLM-related vulnerabilities can be identified, mapped to specific files, or associated with particular model APIs. This assessment is limited by the lack of implementation detail and should
watchdog.recommendation: 1) When introducing any LLMs (e.g., GPT-4o or Claude) into VeriMedia AI, design prompts with strict instruction–data separation and apply the OWASP LLM Top 10 from the outset. 2) Implement a defensive tool-calling layer if LLMs are ever allowed to call moderation APIs, databases, or social media APIs, including input validation and authorization checks for each tool. 3) Once real LLM integration code exists, perform a full code-based AI security review with concrete file/line analysis to detect

top_findings: Insufficient source code provided for security analysis
sentinel.summary: VeriMedia AI is described as a Flask-based AI media verification platform using PostgreSQL, AWS, and REST APIs with file upload processing. No source code was provided, making concrete security analysis impossible. The described architecture suggests a high-risk attack surface that warrants thorough review once code is available.
sentinel.recommendation: 1. Provide the complete source code repository including all application code, configuration files, and dependency manifests so that a meaningful security assessment can be performed. 2. Prioritize sharing Flask route definitions, database access layers, file upload handlers, and authentication middleware as these represent the highest-risk components based on the application description. 3. Include any infrastructure-as-code and deployment configurations for AWS to assess network exposure and s

guardian.summary: VeriMedia AI is a high-risk AI-powered media verification and content moderation platform using ResNet-based deepfake detection and BERT-based fact-verification to make autonomous judgements about content authenticity across Twitter, Facebook, and Reddit. The application has a critically deficient governance posture: it has zero documentation of any kind (no README, no model cards, no privacy policy, no governance policies), no bias or fairness testing despite processing demographic-sensitive co
guardian.recommendation: 1. IMMEDIATE — Halt autonomous content moderation decisions and implement mandatory human review for all verdicts until EU AI Act conformity controls are in place (addresses Finding: 'EU AI Act High-Risk Classification with Zero Conformity Controls' — EUAI-RISK-CLASS; and 'No Human Oversight Mechanism' — EUAI-OVERSIGHT): commission a Fundamental Rights Impact Assessment, appoint an AI System Owner and Data Protection Officer, and document a conformity assessment roadmap within 30 days. 2.
